DISCOVERY 612, NO APPEARANCE. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO COMPEL PLAINTIFF'S ATTENDANCE AT DEPOSITION IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF IS ORDERED TO APPEAR FOR HER DEPOSITION ON THE PREVIOUSLY AGREED UPON DATES OF NOVEMBER 8-9, 2004 ON THE ISSUES REGARDING THE ALLEGED VIOLATIONS OF COURT ORDERS. PLAINTIFF IS FURTHER ORDERED TO APPEAR FOR HER DEPOSITION AS TO ALL OTHER ISSUES ON OR ABOUT DECEMBER 6-10, 2004 ON DATES TO BE AGREED UPON AFTER MEET AND CONFER. ALL REQUESTS FOR SANCTIONS ARE DENIED. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

DISCOVERY 612, JILL B. ROWE APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF SWAIN. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES, SET ONE, IS MOOT AS RESPONSES HAVE BEEN SERVED. THE COURT WILL NOT DETERMINE THE ADEQUACY OF THOSE RESPONSES ON THIS MOTION. THE COURT FINDS THAT THE LATE RESPONSES WERE A MISUSE OF THE DISCOVERY PROCESS AND THAT THERE ARE NO CIRCUMSTANCES WHICH WOULD MAKE THE IMPOSITION OF SANCTIONS UNJUST. SANCTIONS ARE AWARDED AGAINST DEFENDANT IN THE AMOUNT OF $698.80, TO BE PAID WITHIN 30 DAYS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. (612/EAHEWLETT)
